golden_categories.name: Standard FQDN Validation
golden_categories.description: Validates fully qualified domain names (FQDN) under default settings.

golden_categories.name: Trailing Dot Option
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with the option to allow trailing dots.

golden_categories.name: Non-TLD Validation
golden_categories.description: Handles FQDN validation when the requirement for a top-level domain (TLD) is disabled.

golden_categories.name: Numeric TLD Validation
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with numeric TLDs when the requirement for a TLD is disabled.

golden_categories.name: Wildcard Domain Validation
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with wildcard domains enabled.

golden_categories.name: Combined Options Validation
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with multiple options enabled, including trailing dots, underscores, and numeric TLDs.

generated_categories.name: Default and Basic Validations
generated_categories.description: Tests covering the default behavior and basic validation rules for FQDNs, including invalid characters, spaces, and hyphens.

generated_categories.name: Underscore Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests related to the handling of underscores in domain parts, including allowance and rejection scenarios.

generated_categories.name: Trailing Dot Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests focusing on the behavior of trailing dots in domain names, including allowance scenarios.

generated_categories.name: Numeric TLD Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests targeting the behavior of numeric TLDs, including allowance and rejection scenarios.

generated_categories.name: Wildcard Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests related to the handling of wildcard domains, including allowance, rejection, and stripping scenarios.

generated_categories.name: Domain Length Validation
generated_categories.description: Tests focusing on the validation of domain parts exceeding the maximum allowed length, including allowance and rejection scenarios.

generated_categories.name: TLD Space Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests targeting the behavior of spaces in TLDs, including scenarios with require_tld enabled and disabled.

golden_to_generated.reason: GENC-1 aligns with the default and basic validation rules for FQDNs, which correspond to GC-1's standard FQDN validation under default settings.
GENC-3 focuses on trailing dot handling, which directly corresponds to GC-2's validation of FQDNs with the trailing dot option.
GENC-7 addresses spaces in TLDs with require_tld enabled and disabled, partially overlapping with GC-3's non-TLD validation when require_tld is disabled.
GENC-4 directly targets numeric TLD handling, which aligns with GC-4's validation of numeric TLDs when the requirement for a TLD is disabled.
GENC-5 focuses on wildcard handling, which corresponds to GC-5's validation of FQDNs with wildcard domains enabled.

GC-6 covers combined options validation, including trailing dots, underscores, and numeric TLDs. GENC-3, GENC-2, and GENC-4 collectively address these aspects but do not fully integrate them into a single category.

generated_to_golden.reason: GENC-1's focus on default and basic validations strongly aligns with GC-1's standard FQDN validation under default settings.
GENC-2 addresses underscore handling, which partially overlaps with GC-6's combined options validation that includes underscores.
GENC-3's focus on trailing dot handling strongly aligns with GC-2 and partially overlaps with GC-6's combined options validation.
GENC-4's numeric TLD handling strongly aligns with GC-4 and partially overlaps with GC-6's combined options validation.
GENC-5's focus on wildcard handling strongly aligns with GC-5's validation of FQDNs with wildcard domains enabled.
GENC-6's focus on domain length validation does not align with any golden category.
GENC-7's focus on spaces in TLDs partially overlaps with GC-3's non-TLD validation when require_tld is disabled.

novel_generated_categories.reason: GENC-6 introduces domain length validation, which is not covered by any golden category.
